result.markdown: --- title: "Encyclopedia of Color Science and Technology" description: "*The second edition of this extensive textbook has been published.*" author: - name: Kevin Mansfield url: https://kpmansfield.github.io/ orcid: 0000-0003-0966-7883 affiliation: UCL IEDE affiliation-url: https://www.ucl.ac.uk/ date: 2024-01-17 categories: [lighting] # self-defined categories format: html: code-fold: show code-summary: "R code" toc: TRUE toc_float: TRUE citation: url: https://kpmansfield.github.io/posts/2024-01-17/ image: pantheon.jpg draft: false # setting this to `true` will prevent your post from appearing on your listing page until you're ready! --- ![The Pantheon](pantheon.jpg){fig-align="center" width="6in"} A new edition of the *Encyclopedia of Color Science and Technology* has been published and I have contributed a Chapter (together with Professor Peter Raynham) on *Architectural Lighting*. ##### Access The Encyclopedia can be accessed at [https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-89862-5](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-89862-5) ##### Code demonstration This is off-topic but is it possible to show *R* code within a blog post? ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyplots) p <- study |> tidyplot(x=treatment,y=score,color=treatment) |> add_data_points_beeswarm(shape=2) |> add_mean_bar(alpha=0.4) |> add_sem_errorbar() |> adjust_size(width=8,height=6,unit="cm") p ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Note equations: $$e = mc^2$$
